description: Single source of truth for yen-sei training data curation. Tighten thresholds to shrink the gold pool; loosen to widen it. All code reads from here; no hardcoded thresholds in stages/*.py.

marker_patterns._comment_use_core_constants: When true, MARKER_ONLY_PATTERNS from tools.core.go_teaching_constants is the base set. additional_markers extends it.
marker_patterns._comment_marker_prefix_regex: Stripped from comment START before length scoring. 'Right! RIGHT' → '' after strip.
marker_patterns._comment_min_chars_after_strip: If comment after marker stripping is shorter than this, treat as marker-only.

language._comment_method: 'heuristic' = ASCII ratio + stopword count (no deps). 'wordfreq' = use wordfreq library (must be installed). 'both' = require both to pass.
language._comment_min_ascii_letter_ratio: Fraction of comment chars that are [A-Za-z ]. Filters out CJK-heavy comments cheaply.
language._comment_min_stopword_hits: English function words per 100 chars. ~3.0 is conservative real English text.
language._comment_wordfreq_min_zipf: Used only when method includes 'wordfreq'. Mean Zipf frequency threshold across tokens.

hard_gates._comment_max_stones: Above this is a whole-game record, not a tsumego.
hard_gates._comment_min_variations: Need at least correct + wrong path in the SGF tree to be a teachable puzzle. May be bypassed by prose_fallback below.

prose_fallback._comment: A puzzle that fails min_variations may still pass the teachable_content gate if its prose explains wrong moves inline (the 'classic book' style: Xuan Xuan Qi Jing, Harada, etc.). Both conditions must be met. Set enabled=false to disable and require tree branches.
prose_fallback._comment_phrases: Lowercase substrings. Counted as phrase hits in the cleaned comment text. Tuned for English Go-teaching prose; safely conservative.

hard_gates._comment_max_total_moves: Cap on solution-tree node count. Above this is likely a game record.

tier_rules._comment: Each tier checked top-down. First match wins. All thresholds within a tier are AND-ed (the 'structural path'). A tier may optionally define a 'prose_path' that is an alternative content-shape route for puzzles whose wrong-move teaching lives in PROSE inside one big C[] comment instead of in tree branches (classic-book style: Xuan Xuan Qi Jing, Harada, etc.). Both paths are content-driven and contain no source names.

prose_path._comment: Alternative gold path for prose-rich classic-book style. Wrong-move teaching counted as either tree-branch chars OR in-prose refutation phrases.

gold._comment: Cho-Chikun-grade examples. Long correct explanation, multiple wrong-move analyses, cites techniques, uses causal language.

prose_path._comment: Alternative silver path for solid prose-style teaching that doesn't reach gold prose threshold.

silver._comment: Solid teaching content but less rich. Useful for training at 1x weight.

bronze._comment: Marginal. Held back unless gold+silver pool is too small.

source_overrides._comment: Per-source caps and notes. tier_cap='gold' means this source can reach gold; tier_cap='silver' caps at silver even if signals qualify; tier_cap='drop' excludes entirely.

gogameguru.notes: Pro commentary; small but high quality.

kisvadim-goproblems.notes: Mostly drill content; rarely rich teaching.

gotools.notes: Pure drill markers, zero teaching content.

filename_convention._comment: Tier prefix lets harvest read tier from filename without parsing SGF. Use full words gold_/silver_/bronze_ for clarity.

training_weights._comment: Sample-weight multipliers for refine stage. ALWAYS 1.0 for all tiers - we do NOT upsample. Duplicating examples does not add information; it just over-weights the optimizer's gradient on those rows and inflates apparent dataset size. Tier balance is achieved at INGEST time via bronze_selection.cap_policy. Set a tier weight to 0.0 to exclude that tier entirely from train+val+test.

bronze_selection._comment: Bronze pool is the long tail of qualified puzzles. We keep ALL gold and silver, and selectively admit bronze under criteria + cap so bronze cannot dominate the training distribution. Applied at INGEST time before harvest. Set enabled=false to exclude bronze entirely.
bronze_selection._comment_cap: 'max_of_gold_silver': bronze ingested <= len(gold)+len(silver) so bronze cannot exceed combined high-tier weight. 'fixed': use cap_value. 'unlimited': no cap (not recommended).

sort_formula._comment: Composite quality score for ranking bronze when capped. Higher = kept first. score = a*correct_chars + b*wrong_chars + c*techniques + d*causal + e*english_ratio.

test_sets.description: 200 marker-only puzzles, mixed sources, no reference prose. Tests pure puzzle-solving capability.
100 held-out gold+silver with reference prose. Sanity check vs the marker-only sets.

split_ratios._comment: Test split is no longer drawn from the training pool. Test sets are defined explicitly in test_sets[] above and built from marker-only and held-out gold/silver puzzles. Train/val split applies to the remaining training pool only.

dedup._comment_method: Hash of (board_size, sorted black stones, sorted white stones, player_to_move). On collision, keep highest-tier copy.
